1.11a: Contact whoever issued the mortgage offer or instructions.
1.11b: Contact Group Legal, Yorkshire House, Yorkshire Drive, Bradford BD5 8LJ.
The Society's own form of Deed of Postponement must be used.
1.7: Lending is prohibited when the borrower and mortgagor are not the same person.
1.14: The firm may act if the seller is staff or family provided no conflict of interest exists.
The firm must be a partnership.
The specific conveyancer acting must not be the seller or a member of the seller's immediate family.
1.15: The firm may act if the borrower is staff or family provided no conflict of interest exists.
The firm must be a partnership.
The specific conveyancer acting must not be the borrower or a member of the borrower's immediate family.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.1.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is the party who issued the offer or instructions.
3.1.5: Acceptable ID: Full photocard or old style Driving Licence.
Acceptable ID: EU Residence Permit with country passport.
Acceptable ID: EU member state ID card.
Acceptable ID: Foreign National ID card with proof of indefinite leave to remain.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.2.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is the party who issued the offer or instructions.
4.5a: Contact for re-inspection is the party who issued the offer or instructions.
4.5b: The Certificate of Title should be sent to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
4.1: A valuation report is usually provided if a physical interior inspection was performed.
Borrowers who pay a valuation fee will normally receive a copy.
4.3: Incorrect valuation assumptions must be reported to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.1.1: Report properties owned by the seller for less than 6 months to the party who issued instructions.
5.2.1: Report instances where the seller is not the owner/registered proprietor to the party who issued instructions.
5.4.4: Environmental and contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm follows the PCCB Search Code.
Conveyancer must provide an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to Part 1 requirements and an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Indemnity insurance is permitted for planning/building regulation breaches 10+ years old if the valuation is clear.
Breaches within the enforcement period require confirmation of structural soundness and security suitability.
Private drainage systems must be legislation-compliant and registered where required.
Shared drainage systems require legal arrangements for management, access, and maintenance costs.
5.5.3b: Submission of original or copy planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Submission of building regulation consents is not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use are required for commercial-to-residential conversions if planning permission cannot be confirmed.
In other cases, certificates should be retained by the borrower.
5.5.4: Restrictions on marketability must be reported to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they only affect part of the property.
Title must include enforceable rights for support, shelter, repair, and entry.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.7.1c: Report flying freeholds to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.8.1: Buildings with max 4 flats accepted if borrower occupies one and owns/shares the freehold.
The security must be a leasehold flat with a separate title.
If the borrower owns the freehold, it must be included in the security.
Lending is limited to one flat per building.
5.8.5: Security must include the leasehold interest and the freehold reversion of other flats.
Shares in freehold must be transferred simultaneously with the leasehold if the lender enforces security.
A trust deed is required if simultaneous transfer cannot be confirmed.
5.9.1: Lending on commonhold property is not permitted.
5.10.1: Restrictions on use must be reported to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.13.1: Report cases where the borrower is not providing the full balance or is giving a second charge to the offer issuer.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee/insolvent landlords is accepted with an unqualified Certificate of Title.
Conveyancers must retain a copy of the policy on file.
5.14.17a: Documentation is generally not required prior to completion except for building safety matters.
5.14.17b: Lender will not lend on unremediated properties where fire safety works are required and leaseholders may bear costs.
Conveyancers must confirm costs for non-qualifying leaseholders if remediation is needed.
Specific instructions are required if building safety issues exist or remediation is identified.
5.14.17c: Building safety requirements apply to remortgages; 'borrower' replaces 'vendors’ conveyancer' in instructions.
5.14.1: Minimum acceptable unexpired lease term is 85 years from the date of completion.
5.14.9: Maximum starting ground rent is £1000 per annum.
Ground rent must not increase during the first 21 years of the lease.
Reviews must not occur more frequently than every 21 years.
Rent increases are capped at the higher of 100% of the previous rent or the RPI percentage change.
5.14.10: Service charge matters should be reported to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted with an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.14.13: Notice of service or evidence of receipts do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
5.15.2a: Rentcharges/service charges must include a 2-month notice period to the lender before enforcement action.
The recipient must notify the lender in writing of any proposed action regarding non-payment.
Leases created by enforcement must be surrendrable upon payment of arrears/costs without a premium.
Rentcharges must not double more frequently than every 25 years.
Covenants must exist for the performance of maintenance/services in return for charges.
Indemnity insurance is only acceptable for historic, non-service related charges uncollected for 12+ years.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association of the management company do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Contact point for search entry certification issues is the party who issued instructions.
5.17.5a: Power of attorney documents do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation do not need to be sent to the lender.
5.19.1: Lending is not available for shared equity or shared ownership schemes.
5.20.1: Solar panel leases failing UK Finance minimum requirements must be reported to the offer issuer.
5.20.3: Solar PV leases must include a break clause making the provider liable for removal costs and damage repairs if the lender terminates.
5.20.4: Conveyancers must disclose details of any existing Green Deal Plans to the party who issued instructions.
6.1.3: Notify the party who issued the offer if the borrower does not take up the mortgage.
6.2.1: Report discrepancies in the property description to the party who issued instructions.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the party who issued the offer or instructions.
6.4.4: UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives Form is required for all new builds, renovations, or conversions.
The form must be submitted before the Certificate of Title.
6.4.5: Report any lack of control over purchase money to the party who issued instructions.
6.5.1: Report lack of vacant possession or borrower's intention to let to the party who issued instructions immediately.
6.6.1: Contact the offer issuer regarding letting status or to verify buy-to-let eligibility.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements do not need to be sent to the lender.
6.6.4: HMO requirements will be specified in the mortgage offer if lending is approved.
6.7.1: Accepted warranties: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, LABC, Checkmate/Castle 10, Build-Zone, Build Assure, Global Home Warranties, Q Assure Build, Protek, Advantage, ICW, Ark Residential, One Guarantee, TMSC, HomeProof.
Specific endorsements required for detached garages (Checkmate/Ark) and flat roofs > 10sqm (ICW).
6.7.2: Warranty documentation should not be sent but must be retained on file.
6.7.4: Professional Consultant's Certificates are accepted using the UK Finance PCC form.
Max 4 units in a continuous structure; max 10 units in the total development.
Retrospective certificates are not accepted; work must have been supervised from the start.
6.7.6: Professional Consultant's Certificates should not be sent but must be retained on file.
6.8.1: Notification of unadopted road/sewer agreements or bonds is not required.
Lender does not require retentions for unadopted infrastructure.
6.9.1: Missing easements require indemnity insurance to be retained on file.
Lender will not accept charges of part; adjoining land used for access/services must also be charged.
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower.
Advance instalments are released to the conveyancer.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the party who issued the offer.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options to the party who issued instructions.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants to the party who issued instructions.
7.3: Occupants aged 17+ must provide consent, which is incorporated in Mortgage Deed LF 0002.
8.1: Conveyancers may advise third parties if no conflict exists and the document remains legally robust.
9.1: Indemnity policies do not need to be sent but must be retained on file.
9.2: Indemnity limit must at least equal the mortgage advance.
Policy must protect the borrower, successors, and the mortgagee.
10.2a: Mortgage advance is paid electronically via CHAPS.
10.2b: Notice required: 6 working days where no final inspection is needed.
Notice required: 11 working days (6 + 5) if a final inspection is required.
Funds are transmitted on the last working day prior to completion.
10.3: Deductions include Funds Transfer Fee and Higher Lending Charge if applicable.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned electronically to NatWest, Sort Code 56-00-36, Account 78735173.
Funds must be returned within one working day of the completion date.
Interest is charged if funds are not returned or borrower details are missing.
10.11: Interest for delayed return is charged at the borrower's mortgage rate.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is required for instalment mortgages or retentions.
14.1.5: If using e-DRS: Send original mortgage deed and retain certified copy.
If not using e-DRS: Lodge original mortgage deed with the Land Registry.
14.2.1: Specified documents should be sent to: Securities Team, Mortgage Service, Yorkshire House, Yorkshire Drive, Bradford BD5 8LJ (DX 11798 Bradford).
14.2.2: Post-completion documents: Official copy of register, filed plan, and original/certified mortgage deed.
16.1.1: Title documents can be requested from the Securities Team or online at www.ybs.co.uk/legal/lawyers/html.
16.3.7a: Execution of transfer of equity is handled by the Technical Team, Mortgage Service, Bradford BD5 8LJ.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: 'Executed as a deed by affixing the Seal of Yorkshire Building Society in the presence of: By Authority of the Board of Directors'.
16.3.1: Standard deed of covenant is used for changes in mortgage parties.
Releases are issued via separate forms on request.
16.3.2: Debt amount inquiries: Customer Contact Centre, 0345 1 200 200.
16.3.4: Retain a certified copy of the transfer of equity on file if it involves a new borrower or release.
16.4.1: Consent to letting requests: Technical Team, Mortgage Service, Yorkshire House, Yorkshire Drive, Bradford BD5 8LJ.
16.4.2: A copy of the proposed tenancy agreement must be sent to the lender.
16.5.3a-d: Deeds of variation, rectification, easement, and option agreements must be sent to the Technical Team.
17.1.1: Redemption statements must be requested online at the YBS website.
17.2.1a: Discharge and repayment remittances should be sent according to redemption statement instructions.
17.2.1b: The Society registers the mortgage discharge directly with the Land Registry.
